content: If we frame the empirical comparison between intrinsic curiosity methods (ICM, RND) and hand-designed curious agents as an active learning task for neural architecture search, we can evolve episodic memory-augmented curiosity architectures that outperform both hand-tuned agents and fixed baseline methods by dynamically selecting which intrinsic reward signal to use based on current environment uncertainty. This creates a self-improving curiosity loop where the agent’s own architecture adapts its intrinsic motivation mechanism rather than relying on a static intrinsic reward design.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
